title: Amendment to the Bank Secrecy Act Regulations Relating to Orders for Transmittals of Funds by Financial Institutions
abstract: In January 1995, the Department of the Treasury (Treasury), through its Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN), adopted a final rule (60 FR 234, January 3, 1995) requiring financial institutions that transmit funds to include in transmittal orders certain information (the travel rule). On the same date, Treasury, through FinCEN, and the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System (the Board) jointly adopted a final rule (60 FR 220, January 3, 1995) requiring financial institutions to
action: Final rule; delay of effective date.
issuing_agency: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
